items.title: Cold Intro Email
items.prompt: Write a concise, personalized cold email introducing myself to [Name] at [Company]. My name is [Your Name], I work at [Your Company], and I want to explore [specific opportunity or collaboration]. Keep it under 120 words, lead with a genuine compliment about their work, and end with a low-friction ask like a 15-minute call.

items.title: Follow-Up After No Reply
items.prompt: Write a polite, non-pushy follow-up email to [Name] who hasn't responded to my message from [X days ago] about [topic]. Keep it under 60 words. Reference the original message briefly, restate the value clearly, and offer an easy way to decline if they're not interested.

items.title: Job Application Cover Email
items.prompt: Write a professional cover email for a [Job Title] position at [Company Name]. I have [X years] of experience in [field], my top relevant accomplishments are [accomplishment 1] and [accomplishment 2], and I'm excited about this role because [specific reason tied to the company's mission or product]. Keep it to 4 short paragraphs.

items.title: Networking Coffee Chat Request
items.prompt: Write a warm, respectful email requesting a 20-minute virtual coffee chat with [Name], who works in [role/industry] at [Company]. I found them through [LinkedIn/event/mutual connection]. I want to learn about [specific topic]. Be genuine, not transactional, and make it easy for them to say yes or no.

items.title: Business Proposal Email
items.prompt: Write a compelling email proposing [service or partnership] to [Company Name]. Our solution helps companies like theirs achieve [specific outcome] — recent clients saw [measurable result]. Keep it under 150 words. Include a clear subject line, one-sentence value proposition, three bullet-point benefits, and a specific call to action.

items.title: Professional Apology Email
items.prompt: Write a professional apology email to [Name] acknowledging that [describe the mistake or delay]. Take full responsibility without over-explaining. Briefly describe what I'm doing to fix it and prevent recurrence. End with a reassurance of my commitment to the relationship. Keep it sincere and under 100 words.

items.title: Salary Negotiation Email
items.prompt: Write a confident, professional email negotiating the salary for a [Job Title] offer from [Company]. I received an offer of [amount]. Based on my [X years] of experience, my skills in [specific areas], and market data showing the range for this role is [range], I'd like to request [target amount]. Express genuine enthusiasm for the role throughout.

items.title: Client Project Update
items.prompt: Write a clear client-facing project update email for [Project Name]. This week we completed [accomplishments]. Next we'll be working on [upcoming milestones]. Current status is [on track/slightly delayed] because [brief reason]. Include any decisions we need from the client. Professional, concise, no filler.

items.title: LinkedIn Story Post
items.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post that shares a lesson I learned from [experience or challenge]. Start with a one-line hook that stops the scroll. Share the story briefly (3–4 sentences), then the specific lesson, then a takeaway for others in [industry or profession]. Use short paragraphs, no hashtag spam. End with an engaging question to spark comments.

items.title: Twitter/X Thread
items.prompt: Write a 7-tweet thread about [topic]. Tweet 1: a bold, attention-grabbing claim or question. Tweets 2–6: one specific, actionable insight each, explained clearly. Tweet 7: a summary and call to follow for more. Each tweet under 260 characters. No filler. Use numbers and concrete examples.

items.title: Instagram Caption
items.prompt: Write an engaging Instagram caption for a photo of [describe the photo]. The tone should be [casual/inspirational/humorous]. Start with a strong first line that appears before the 'more' cut-off. Tell a brief story or share a thought, then end with a question or call to action to encourage comments. Include 5 relevant hashtags at the end.

items.title: Blog Post Introduction
items.prompt: Write a compelling introduction for a blog post titled '[Post Title]' aimed at [target audience]. Open with a relatable pain point or surprising statistic. Then pivot to the promise of what they'll learn. Keep it under 120 words. Avoid throat-clearing phrases like 'In this post, I will...' — just dive in.

items.title: YouTube Video Script Outline
items.prompt: Create a detailed script outline for a [X]-minute YouTube video about [topic]. Include: a hook (first 15 seconds), the main promise, 3–4 clearly labeled sections with talking points and B-roll suggestions, a mid-video engagement moment, and a strong outro with call to action. Target audience: [audience description].

items.title: Email Newsletter Section
items.prompt: Write one section of a weekly email newsletter for [audience type] about [topic]. The tone is [conversational/professional/witty]. Include a short anecdote or insight (3–4 sentences), one actionable tip, and a recommendation (tool, article, or resource) with a one-line explanation of why it's worth their time.

items.title: Product Description
items.prompt: Write a compelling product description for [Product Name], a [product type] that [core benefit]. Target customer: [describe them]. Lead with the transformation it creates, not the features. Then list 4 key features framed as benefits. End with a confidence-building sentence. Tone: [approachable/premium/exciting]. Max 150 words.

items.title: Resume Bullet Points
items.prompt: Rewrite these job responsibilities as strong resume bullet points using the STAR format (Situation, Task, Action, Result). Start each with a powerful action verb. Add specific metrics where possible. Role: [Job Title]. Company: [Company]. Responsibilities to rewrite: [paste your bullets here].

items.title: LinkedIn About Section
items.prompt: Write my LinkedIn About section. I'm a [job title] with [X years] experience in [industry]. My expertise includes [top 3 skills]. I'm most proud of [career highlight]. I'm passionate about [what drives you]. Currently I'm [what you're doing/looking for]. Write it in first person, conversational but professional, 150–200 words. End with a call to connect.

items.title: Interview Answer (STAR Method)
items.prompt: Write a strong, concise answer to the interview question: '[Question]'. Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result). The role I'm interviewing for is [Job Title] at [Company]. My relevant experience includes [briefly describe]. Keep it under 2 minutes when spoken aloud. Sound confident, not scripted.

items.title: Self-Performance Review
items.prompt: Help me write my self-assessment for my annual performance review. This year, my key accomplishments were: [list them]. Areas I grew in: [list]. Challenges I faced: [list]. Goals for next year: [list]. Write it in a confident, professional tone that highlights impact without sounding arrogant. About 300 words.

items.title: Promotion Request Message
items.prompt: Write a professional message to my manager [Name] requesting a conversation about a promotion from [Current Role] to [Target Role]. I've been in my current role for [X time]. My key contributions this year include [accomplishments]. Reference how my scope has expanded beyond my current title. Keep it assertive but collaborative, under 150 words.

items.title: Reference Request Email
items.prompt: Write an email asking [Name], my former [manager/colleague/mentor], to be a professional reference for a [Job Title] role at [Company]. Briefly remind them of the projects we worked on together and the skills that are most relevant. Make it easy for them to say yes. Attach a summary of the role so they're prepared.

items.title: Resignation Letter
items.prompt: Write a professional, gracious resignation letter to my manager [Name]. I'm resigning from [Job Title] at [Company], effective [date, giving X weeks notice]. I want to express genuine gratitude for the opportunities, offer to help with the transition, and leave on excellent terms. Keep it warm, brief, and professional. No burning bridges.

items.title: Pitch Deck Narrative
items.prompt: Write the narrative for a [X]-slide pitch deck for [Company/Product Name]. We're a [describe in one sentence]. The problem we solve: [problem]. Our solution: [solution]. Market size: [TAM/SAM]. Business model: [how you make money]. Traction so far: [metrics]. Why us: [unfair advantage]. The ask: [how much, for what]. Make it investor-ready and compelling.

items.title: Meeting Agenda
items.prompt: Create a structured agenda for a [X]-minute [type of meeting] with [attendees/teams]. The goal of this meeting is to [desired outcome]. Topics to cover: [list topics]. Format each item with a time allocation, owner, and clear objective. Add a 5-minute buffer and include pre-read materials if needed. Meeting date: [date].

items.title: SWOT Analysis Framework
items.prompt: Conduct a SWOT analysis for [Company/Product/Initiative]. For each quadrant, give 4–5 specific, non-generic points. Strengths and Weaknesses should be internal. Opportunities and Threats should be external and market-driven. After the matrix, add a 2–3 sentence strategic synthesis: given this SWOT, where should the focus be in the next 90 days?

items.title: OKR Framework
items.prompt: Write OKRs for [team or company] for [Q/Year]. I want to focus on [strategic priority]. Write 2–3 Objectives. Under each Objective, write 3 measurable Key Results using the format 'Increase/Decrease/Launch X from Y to Z by [date]'. Objectives should be inspiring and qualitative. Key Results must be quantitative and unambiguous.

items.title: Competitor Analysis Brief
items.prompt: Write a competitive analysis brief comparing [My Company/Product] to [Competitor 1] and [Competitor 2] across these dimensions: target customer, pricing model, key features, positioning, strengths, and weaknesses. End with a section on our differentiation opportunities. Present in a scannable format a leadership team could read in 5 minutes.

items.title: Product Launch Press Release
items.prompt: Write a press release announcing the launch of [Product/Feature Name] by [Company Name]. Follow AP style. Include: dateline, headline, compelling lead paragraph (who/what/when/where/why), a quote from [executive name and title], two paragraphs of product detail and benefits, a second quote from [customer/partner], and a standard boilerplate about the company. Contact: [name, email].

items.title: Executive Summary
items.prompt: Write an executive summary for a [type of document: business plan/report/proposal] about [topic]. Length: 200–250 words. Cover: the problem or opportunity, the proposed solution or recommendation, key evidence or data points, expected outcomes or ROI, and the requested action or decision. Start with the most important insight, not background context.

items.title: Short Story Opening
items.prompt: Write the opening 200 words of a short story with the following setup: genre is [genre], main character is [brief description], the setting is [place and time], and the central tension or mystery that will drive the story is [conflict]. Start in the middle of action or a striking sensory detail. No preamble. Make me want to keep reading.

items.title: Modern Poem
items.prompt: Write a contemporary free-verse poem about [theme or subject]. The emotional tone should be [melancholic/joyful/tense/nostalgic]. Use concrete, specific imagery — avoid clichés. Length: 12–18 lines. Aim for a memorable final image or line that reframes everything before it. Do not rhyme unless it happens naturally.

items.title: Wedding Speech
items.prompt: Write a heartfelt, funny, and memorable wedding speech for [your role: best man/maid of honor/parent]. The couple is [Name 1] and [Name 2]. Key stories or memories to include: [list 2–3]. A running theme or inside joke: [optional]. Length: 3–4 minutes when spoken. Open with a laugh, build to a genuine emotional moment, end with the toast.

items.title: Toast or Tribute
items.prompt: Write a 60-second toast honoring [Name] for [occasion: retirement/birthday/award/promotion]. This person is known for [2–3 qualities]. A specific moment that captures who they are: [anecdote]. Include genuine warmth, one light-hearted moment, and a memorable closing line for the toast. Suitable to say in front of [professional/family/close friends] audience.

items.title: Personal Statement
items.prompt: Write a compelling personal statement for [college/graduate school/scholarship/fellowship] application. I am applying for [program] at [institution]. My background: [key experiences]. My defining moment or turning point: [story]. Why this specific program: [specific reasons tied to the curriculum, faculty, or mission]. My goal: [what I want to do with this]. 500 words, first person, authentic voice.

items.title: Third-Person Speaker Bio
items.prompt: Write a polished third-person bio for [Name] to be used for [conference/podcast/website]. They are a [title] who [core expertise in one sentence]. Notable achievements: [list 2–3]. They've been featured in or worked with [brands/publications/organizations]. Currently: [what they're working on]. Personal note: [hobby or interest that humanizes them]. Length: 100 words for short version, 200 words for long.

items.title: Weekly Review Template
items.prompt: Help me do a structured weekly review. Ask me the following (one at a time or all at once): What were my biggest wins this week? What didn't get done, and why? What drained my energy? What gave me energy? What's the one thing I need to prioritize next week? Then summarize my answers into a clear 'start/stop/continue' and a top-3 priority list for next week.

items.title: Project Brief
items.prompt: Write a one-page project brief for [Project Name]. Sections to include: Background (why this project exists), Goal (what success looks like in measurable terms), Scope (what's in and out of scope), Key Deliverables with owners and due dates, Dependencies and risks, Stakeholders and decision-makers, and Budget/Resources required. Keep each section to 2–4 sentences.

items.title: Daily Prioritization Plan
items.prompt: Help me plan my day. I have these tasks: [paste your task list]. My working hours are [start]–[end]. My must-do appointment or meeting is at [time]. I work best on deep work in the [morning/afternoon]. Group similar tasks, protect 2 hours for my most important project, batch emails/messages into one slot, and leave a 30-minute buffer. Give me a time-blocked schedule.

items.title: Standard Operating Procedure
items.prompt: Write a clear Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for [process name] at [company type]. The process goal: [what it accomplishes]. Audience: [who will use it]. Format: numbered steps, clear action verbs, decision points highlighted in bold. Include: purpose, scope, prerequisites, step-by-step instructions, common mistakes to avoid, and revision date. Keep technical jargon minimal.

items.title: Team Retrospective
items.prompt: Facilitate a sprint/project retrospective for [team name] on [project or sprint name]. Duration: [X] weeks. Use the 4Ls framework: Liked (what went well), Learned (new insights), Lacked (what was missing), Longed For (what we wished we had). For each section, write 3–4 starter prompts to get the team talking. End with a template for capturing 3 action items with owners.

items.title: Decision Framework
items.prompt: Help me make a structured decision about [decision I'm facing]. I need to choose between: [Option A], [Option B], [Option C if applicable]. The most important factors to me are: [list 3–5 criteria]. Constraints: [time, budget, risk tolerance]. Create a weighted decision matrix, score each option against my criteria, and give a recommendation with a 2-sentence rationale. Then tell me the top risk of the recommended option.

items.title: Habit Building Plan
items.prompt: Create a realistic 30-day plan to build the habit of [habit]. My current behavior: [what I do now]. My goal: [what I want to do consistently]. My biggest obstacle: [what gets in the way]. Use implementation intention format ('I will [behavior] at [time] in [location]'). Include a weekly check-in structure, a way to track streaks, and what to do when I miss a day.
